symbols.name: decoder

symbols.name: positive_scores

symbols.doc: Per-class differential scores `I+_k - I-_k`.

symbols.name: diff_features
symbols.doc: Raw `2K` region integrals as a feature vector, interleaved `[I+_0, I-_0, I+_1, I-_1, ...]`. This is the differential readout's full information (before the per-class subtraction) and is what a small trainable decoder consumes. `plain_features` exposes only the `K` positive integr

symbols.name: diff_features
symbols.doc: Raw `2K` region integrals as a feature vector, interleaved `[I+_0, I-_0, I+_1, I-_1, ...]`. This is the differential readout's full information (before the per-class subtraction) and is what a small trainable decoder consumes. `plain_features` exposes only the `K` positive integr

symbols.name: plain_features
symbols.doc: The `K` positive-region integrals only (plain readout feature set).

symbols.name: plain_features
symbols.doc: The `K` positive-region integrals only (plain readout feature set).

symbols.name: predict_plain
symbols.doc: Plain prediction: argmax of the positive-region integrals only. Reads `num_classes` regions.

symbols.name: predict_plain
symbols.doc: Plain prediction: argmax of the positive-region integrals only. Reads `num_classes` regions.

symbols.name: predict_differential
symbols.doc: Differential prediction: argmax of `I+_k - I-_k`. Reads `2 * num_classes` regions.

symbols.name: predict_differential
symbols.doc: Differential prediction: argmax of `I+_k - I-_k`. Reads `2 * num_classes` regions.

symbols.name: grad_adam
symbols.doc: Deterministic numeric primitives for gradient training: Adam, average pooling, L2-normalize, softmax.

symbols.name: Pooling
symbols.doc: Average-pool a row-major `w×h` grid to `s×s`, replicating [`crate::decoder::pool_features`]'s exact box boundaries. Stores each output cell's source indices + `1/count` weight so the backward pass can scatter `∂L/∂f_raw` onto pixels.

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: Pooling::fn new(w: usize, h: usize, sensor: usize) -> Self

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: fn new(w: usize, h: usize, sensor: usize) -> Self

symbols.name: forward
symbols.doc: Forward pool: `intensity` (w*h) -> raw pooled means (s*s).

symbols.name: forward
symbols.doc: Forward pool: `intensity` (w*h) -> raw pooled means (s*s).

symbols.name: l2_normalize
symbols.doc: L2-normalize in place, returning the pre-norm length (1.0 if degenerate) so the backward pass can use it. Matches `decoder::pool_features`'s 1e-9 guard.

symbols.name: softmax
symbols.doc: Numerically-stable softmax (subtract max), fixed order.

symbols.name: Adam
symbols.doc: Adam state for one parameter vector. `step` mutates the vector in place.

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: Adam::fn new(n: usize, lr: f32) -> Self
symbols.doc: Zero-initialized moments for an `n`-length parameter vector at rate `lr`, with the textbook `eps=1e-8`. Use [`Adam::with_eps`] to override `eps` (e.g. `1e-7` to avoid `v_hat` underflow freezing near-zero-grad cells).

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: fn new(n: usize, lr: f32) -> Self
symbols.doc: Zero-initialized moments for an `n`-length parameter vector at rate `lr`, with the textbook `eps=1e-8`. Use [`Adam::with_eps`] to override `eps` (e.g. `1e-7` to avoid `v_hat` underflow freezing near-zero-grad cells).

symbols.name: with_eps
symbols.signature: Adam::fn with_eps(n: usize, lr: f32, eps: f32) -> Self
symbols.doc: As [`Adam::new`] but with an explicit `eps`.

symbols.name: with_eps
symbols.signature: fn with_eps(n: usize, lr: f32, eps: f32) -> Self
symbols.doc: As [`Adam::new`] but with an explicit `eps`.

symbols.name: step
symbols.doc: In-place Adam step on `param` given `grad` (same length). Fixed index order, no FMA — identical results across runs.

symbols.name: step
symbols.doc: In-place Adam step on `param` given `grad` (same length). Fixed index order, no FMA — identical results across runs.

symbols.name: grad_cascade
symbols.doc: Multi-plane diffractive cascade trained end-to-end by gradient descent (a small D2NN), built on the PROVEN single-plane pieces (ADR-260 roadmap).

symbols.name: Cascade
symbols.signature: struct Cascade { prop: &Propagator, theta: Vec<Vec<f32>>, n: usize }
symbols.doc: Per-plane phase planes of a cascade, plus the shared optical operator. `theta[i]` is the full-grid (`w*h`) phase of plane `i+1` in the forward order.

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: Cascade::fn new(prop: &Propagator, theta: Vec<Vec<f32>>, n: usize) -> Self

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: fn new(prop: &Propagator, theta: Vec<Vec<f32>>, n: usize) -> Self

symbols.name: planes
symbols.doc: Number of cascaded phase planes.

symbols.name: planes
symbols.doc: Number of cascaded phase planes.

symbols.name: forward_fields
symbols.doc: Forward the cascade, returning the per-plane masked fields `v_i` (`v[i] = u_{i-1} ⊙ e^{iθ_i}`, kept for the backward phase rule) and the final field `u_K`. `u_0` is the incident field (image amplitude).

symbols.name: forward_fields
symbols.doc: Forward the cascade, returning the per-plane masked fields `v_i` (`v[i] = u_{i-1} ⊙ e^{iθ_i}`, kept for the backward phase rule) and the final field `u_K`. `u_0` is the incident field (image amplitude).

symbols.name: intensity
symbols.doc: Sensor intensity `I = |u_K|^2` for a sample (the readout the head/NCC see).

symbols.name: intensity
symbols.doc: Sensor intensity `I = |u_K|^2` for a sample (the readout the head/NCC see).

symbols.name: phase_grads
symbols.signature: Cascade::fn phase_grads(self: &Self, v_planes: &[Vec<Complex>], u_k: &[Complex], w_pix: &[f32]) -> Vec<Vec<f32>>
symbols.doc: Backprop a sensor-plane per-pixel weight `w_pix = ∂L/∂I` to each plane's phase gradient, COMPOSING the proven adjoint. Returns `K` gradient vectors (`grad[i] = ∂L/∂θ_i`, same forward order as `self.theta`).

symbols.name: phase_grads
symbols.signature: fn phase_grads(self: &Self, v_planes: &[Vec<Complex>], u_k: &[Complex], w_pix: &[f32]) -> Vec<Vec<f32>>
symbols.doc: Backprop a sensor-plane per-pixel weight `w_pix = ∂L/∂I` to each plane's phase gradient, COMPOSING the proven adjoint. Returns `K` gradient vectors (`grad[i] = ∂L/∂θ_i`, same forward order as `self.theta`).

symbols.name: head_forward_backward
symbols.signature: fn head_forward_backward(intensity: &[f32], pool: &Pooling, head: &LinearHead, label: usize, raw_pool: bool, grad_w: &mut [f32], grad_b: &mut [f32]) -> (f32, Vec<f32>)
symbols.doc: Forward the differentiable head over the cascade's sensor readout and return `(loss, w_pix = ∂L/∂I)` while accumulating the head's `∂L/∂W,∂L/∂b`. The digital tail (CE→softmax→head→(L2-norm)→avg-pool→∂L/∂I) is identical to the single-plane [`crate::grad_train::sample_forward_backw

symbols.name: CascadeSample
symbols.signature: struct CascadeSample { u0: Vec<Complex>, label: usize }
symbols.doc: One sample's incident field + label (mirrors [`crate::grad_train::GradSample`]).

symbols.name: CascadeTrainConfig
symbols.signature: struct CascadeTrainConfig { planes: usize, epochs: usize, batch: usize, lr_mask: f32, lr_head: f32, sensor: usize, seed: u64, raw_pool: bool, adam_eps: f32 }
symbols.doc: Hyperparameters for a cascade gradient-training run.

symbols.name: CascadeTrainOutcome
symbols.signature: struct CascadeTrainOutcome { theta: Vec<Vec<f32>>, loss_curve: Vec<f32>, width: usize, height: usize }
symbols.doc: Trained cascade: per-plane phases + per-epoch mean CE.

symbols.name: train_cascade_grad
symbols.signature: fn train_cascade_grad(prop: &Propagator, width: usize, height: usize, data: &[CascadeSample], theta0: &[Vec<f32>], cfg: &CascadeTrainConfig) -> CascadeTrainOutcome
symbols.doc: Train a `K`-plane diffractive cascade by gradient descent on the differentiable CE head, through the PROVEN composed adjoint. `theta0` holds `K` initial phase planes (each length `w*h`); one shared linear head is trained alongside. The minibatch order, reduction order, and Adam u

symbols.name: grad_train
symbols.doc: Gradient-based phase-mask training (the ceiling-break path, ADR-260 roadmap).

symbols.name: LinearHead
symbols.signature: struct LinearHead { w: Vec<f32>, b: Vec<f32>, dim: usize }
symbols.doc: Linear softmax head over the pooled sensor readout. Plain f32, fixed index order; the only trainable digital params (eval discards it for the NCC).

symbols.name: logits
symbols.doc: Logits `z = W·f + b` (length CLASSES), fixed accumulation order.

symbols.name: logits
symbols.doc: Logits `z = W·f + b` (length CLASSES), fixed accumulation order.

symbols.name: GradSample
symbols.signature: struct GradSample { u0: Vec<Complex>, label: usize }
symbols.doc: One sample's precomputed incident field `u0` (image -> centered amplitude), reused every epoch (it never changes — only θ trains).

symbols.name: build_grad_samples
symbols.doc: Build the per-sample incident fields once. `cfg.width/height` is the grid.

symbols.name: GradTrainConfig
symbols.signature: struct GradTrainConfig { epochs: usize, batch: usize, lr_mask: f32, lr_head: f32, sensor: usize, seed: u64, raw_pool: bool, adam_eps: f32 }
symbols.doc: Hyperparameters for one gradient-training run.

symbols.name: GradTrainOutcome
symbols.signature: struct GradTrainOutcome { theta: Vec<f32>, loss_curve: Vec<f32>, width: usize, height: usize }
symbols.doc: Outcome of gradient training: the trained mask phases + per-epoch mean CE.

symbols.name: train_mask_grad
symbols.signature: fn train_mask_grad(prop: &Propagator, width: usize, height: usize, data: &[GradSample], theta0: &[f32], cfg: &GradTrainConfig) -> GradTrainOutcome
symbols.doc: Train a phase mask by gradient descent on the differentiable CE head.

symbols.doc: In-Rust phase-mask learner (ADR-260 §16.1 "learned optical mask").

symbols.name: LearnConfig

symbols.name: Score
symbols.signature: struct Score { accuracy: f32, margin: f32 }
symbols.doc: Score of a candidate mask: training accuracy with a separation-margin tiebreak. Higher is better; compared lexicographically.

symbols.name: LearnOutcome
symbols.signature: struct LearnOutcome { mask: PhaseMask, decoder: NearestCentroid, start_score: Score, final_score: Score }
symbols.doc: Result of a learning run.

symbols.doc: Train a phase mask + decoder on `train` via seeded block hill-climbing.

symbols.name: photonlayer_bench
symbols.doc: # PhotonLayer Bench

symbols.name: mnist_bench
symbols.doc: Real-data MNIST optical-compression benchmark + differential-detection ablation (ADR-260 M2).

symbols.name: MnistBenchConfig
symbols.signature: struct MnistBenchConfig { grid: usize, cell: usize, sensor: usize, iterations: usize, block: usize, sigma: f32, seed: u64 }
symbols.doc: Configuration for the MNIST differential benchmark.

symbols.name: MnistBenchResult
symbols.signature: struct MnistBenchResult { train_size: usize, test_size: usize, grid: usize, cell: usize, seed: u64, baseline_acc: f32, optical_acc: f32, decoder_params: usize, baseline_decoder_params: usize, optics_only_differential: f32, optics_only_plain: f32, random_optics_only_differential: f32, random_optical_acc: f32, config_b_seed: u64, config_b_plain: f32, config_b_differential: f32, baseline_pixels: usize, optical_sensor_pixels: usize, sensor_reduction_x: f32, baseline_macs: usize, optical_macs: usize, mac_reduction_x: f32 }
symbols.doc: Result of one MNIST benchmark run. Every field is a directly measured number.

symbols.name: acceptance_pass
symbols.doc: The acceptance test (the user's own, relative-to-baseline): optical within 2pp of baseline AND >=16x sensor reduction AND >=10x MACs.

symbols.name: acceptance_pass
symbols.doc: The acceptance test (the user's own, relative-to-baseline): optical within 2pp of baseline AND >=16x sensor reduction AND >=10x MACs.

symbols.name: run_mnist_config_a
symbols.signature: fn run_mnist_config_a(train: &[Sample], test: &[Sample], bcfg: &MnistBenchConfig) -> (f32, f32, f32, f32)
symbols.doc: Config-A-only fast path for tuning the training budget: trains the decoder- objective mask and returns `(baseline_acc, optical_acc, sensor_reduction_x, mac_reduction_x)` without retraining Config B. Used by the iteration sweep.

symbols.name: run_mnist_differential
symbols.doc: Train two masks with two objectives, then run the full acceptance comparison + differential-detection ablation on each. Determinism: every mask is born from a stated seed and the optimizer is seeded, so the whole run is bit- reproducible.

symbols.name: GradMnistResult
symbols.signature: struct GradMnistResult { train_size: usize, test_size: usize, grid: usize, sensor: usize, seed: u64, epochs: usize, lr_mask: f32, grad_optical_acc: f32, random_optical_acc: f32, baseline_acc: f32, loss_curve: Vec<f32>, sensor_reduction_x: f32, baseline_pixels: usize, optical_sensor_pixels: usize }
symbols.doc: Result of the gradient-trained ceiling-break run. Every field is measured.

symbols.name: run_mnist_grad
symbols.signature: fn run_mnist_grad(train: &[Sample], test: &[Sample], grid: usize, sensor: usize, gc: &GradTrainConfig) -> GradMnistResult
symbols.doc: Train a phase mask by GRADIENT DESCENT through the proven diffraction adjoint and evaluate blind-test accuracy with the SAME nearest-centroid decoder the hill-climb baseline uses. This is the ceiling-break measurement.

symbols.doc: Error type for the optical core.

symbols.name: PhotonError
symbols.signature: enum PhotonError { DimensionMismatch, NotPowerOfTwo, InvalidConfig, InvalidMask, Serde }

symbols.name: fft
symbols.doc: Dependency-free, deterministic FFT (iterative radix-2 Cooley–Tukey).

symbols.name: is_pow2
symbols.doc: Returns true if `n` is a power of two and non-zero.

symbols.name: TwiddleTable
symbols.doc: Precomputed twiddle factors for a length-`n` FFT of a fixed direction.

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: TwiddleTable::fn new(n: usize, inverse: bool) -> Self
symbols.doc: Build the table for a length-`n` (power-of-two) transform.

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: fn new(n: usize, inverse: bool) -> Self
symbols.doc: Build the table for a length-`n` (power-of-two) transform.

symbols.name: fft_1d
symbols.doc: In-place 1D FFT. `inverse = true` computes the inverse transform and applies the `1/N` normalization so that `ifft(fft(x)) == x`.

symbols.name: fft_1d_with
symbols.doc: In-place 1D FFT using a precomputed [`TwiddleTable`] (must match the buffer length and direction).

symbols.name: fft_2d
symbols.signature: fn fft_2d(data: &mut [Complex], width: usize, height: usize, inverse: bool)
symbols.doc: In-place 2D FFT on a row-major `width * height` buffer.

symbols.name: checkerboard_premultiply
symbols.signature: fn checkerboard_premultiply(data: &mut [Complex], width: usize, height: usize)
symbols.doc: Checkerboard premultiply: negate every sample at an odd `(row + col)`.

symbols.name: fftshift_2d
symbols.signature: fn fftshift_2d(data: &mut [Complex], width: usize, height: usize)
symbols.doc: 2D fftshift: swaps quadrants so the zero-frequency component moves to the center. `width` and `height` must be even (always true for power-of-two).

symbols.doc: Total optical power `sum |field|^2`. Conserved by lossless propagation.

symbols.name: mask

symbols.doc: A phase-only optical element. One phase delay (radians, `[0, 2π)`) per cell.

symbols.signature: PhaseMask::fn identity(width: usize, height: usize) -> Self
symbols.doc: An identity (flat) mask — equivalent to "optical layer off".

symbols.name: identity
symbols.signature: fn identity(width: usize, height: usize) -> Self
symbols.doc: An identity (flat) mask — equivalent to "optical layer off".

symbols.name: random
symbols.signature: PhaseMask::fn random(width: usize, height: usize, seed: u64) -> Self
symbols.doc: A deterministic pseudo-random phase mask (baseline per ADR-260 §16.1). Same seed => same mask => same hash, satisfying the replay invariant.

symbols.name: random
symbols.signature: fn random(width: usize, height: usize, seed: u64) -> Self
symbols.doc: A deterministic pseudo-random phase mask (baseline per ADR-260 §16.1). Same seed => same mask => same hash, satisfying the replay invariant.

symbols.name: lens
symbols.signature: PhaseMask::fn lens(width: usize, height: usize, focal_strength: f32) -> Self
symbols.doc: A hand-designed quadratic lens phase (baseline "designed lens"). `focal_strength` scales the curvature; positive focuses the field.

symbols.name: lens
symbols.signature: fn lens(width: usize, height: usize, focal_strength: f32) -> Self
symbols.doc: A hand-designed quadratic lens phase (baseline "designed lens"). `focal_strength` scales the curvature; positive focuses the field.

symbols.name: apply
symbols.doc: Apply the mask to a field: `out = field * exp(i * phase)`.

symbols.name: apply
symbols.doc: Apply the mask to a field: `out = field * exp(i * phase)`.

symbols.name: phase_histogram
symbols.doc: A normalized phase histogram (default 16 bins) used as a mask embedding for RuVector experiment memory (ADR-260 §12).

symbols.name: phase_histogram
symbols.doc: A normalized phase histogram (default 16 bins) used as a mask embedding for RuVector experiment memory (ADR-260 §12).

symbols.name: metrics
symbols.doc: Benchmark metrics (ADR-260 §16.2) and embedding helpers (§12).

symbols.name: mse
symbols.doc: Mean-squared error between two equally-sized slices.

symbols.name: psnr
symbols.signature: fn psnr(a: &[f32], b: &[f32], peak: f32) -> f32
symbols.doc: Peak signal-to-noise ratio in dB, assuming a peak value of `peak`.

symbols.name: compression_ratio
symbols.doc: Sensor compression ratio: input pixels / sensor pixels (ADR-260 §16.2).

symbols.name: accuracy
symbols.doc: Classification accuracy over predicted vs. true labels.

symbols.name: input_frame_similarity
symbols.doc: Normalized cross-correlation between an input image and a detector frame, used to demonstrate that the encoded frame is **not** human-readable (ADR-260 acceptance §17.3): a low score means the sensor pattern does not look like the input. Both are resampled to the frame grid by ne

symbols.name: frame_spectrum_embedding
symbols.doc: A radial intensity-spectrum embedding of a detector frame (ADR-260 §12). Produces a fixed-length vector describing how energy is distributed by distance from the frame center — a compact, comparable signature.

symbols.name: MetricReport
symbols.signature: struct MetricReport { accuracy: f32, reconstruction_mse: f32, compression_ratio: f32, input_frame_similarity: f32, native_latency_us: f64 }
symbols.doc: A bundle of benchmark metrics for one experiment, hashable for receipts.

symbols.name: metrics_hash
symbols.doc: Deterministic digest of the metric vector for the receipt (§15).

symbols.name: metrics_hash
symbols.doc: Deterministic digest of the metric vector for the receipt (§15).

symbols.name: propagate
symbols.doc: Scalar diffraction propagation (ADR-260 §9.3).

symbols.name: propagate
symbols.doc: Propagate a field by `config.propagation_mm` using the selected model.

symbols.name: Propagator
symbols.doc: A precomputed propagation operator. Build once per `(config, width, height)` and reuse across many fields — the config-only transfer function is computed a single time instead of on every call. This is the hot path in mask-learning loops (thousands of propagations share one confi

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: Propagator::fn new(width: usize, height: usize, config: &OpticalConfig) -> Result<Self>
symbols.doc: Precompute the operator for a fixed grid + config.

symbols.name: new
symbols.signature: fn new(width: usize, height: usize, config: &OpticalConfig) -> Result<Self>
symbols.doc: Precompute the operator for a fixed grid + config.

symbols.name: propagate
symbols.doc: Propagate a field through the precomputed operator.

symbols.name: propagate
symbols.doc: Propagate a field through the precomputed operator.

symbols.name: propagate_into
symbols.doc: **In-place** propagation — forward FFT → ×H → inverse FFT, mutating `data` directly (no per-call field clone). Bit-identical to [`Propagator::propagate`]; this is the batch hot path (mask-learning loops over many samples).

symbols.name: propagate_into
symbols.doc: **In-place** propagation — forward FFT → ×H → inverse FFT, mutating `data` directly (no per-call field clone). Bit-identical to [`Propagator::propagate`]; this is the batch hot path (mask-learning loops over many samples).

symbols.name: backward_into
symbols.doc: **Adjoint** (conjugate-transpose) of [`Propagator::propagate_into`], in place.

symbols.name: backward_into
symbols.doc: **Adjoint** (conjugate-transpose) of [`Propagator::propagate_into`], in place.

symbols.name: phase_gradient
symbols.signature: fn phase_gradient(prop: &Propagator, u0: &[Complex], theta: &[f32], w_weight: &[f32]) -> Result<Vec<f32>>
symbols.doc: Analytic gradient of the intensity loss `L = Σ_k w[k] · |P(u0 ⊙ e^{iθ})[k]|²` with respect to the per-cell phase `θ`, for the Transfer propagation arm.

symbols.name: intensity_loss
symbols.signature: fn intensity_loss(prop: &Propagator, u0: &[Complex], theta: &[f32], w_weight: &[f32]) -> Result<f32>
symbols.doc: Scalar loss `L = Σ_k w[k] · |P(u0 ⊙ e^{iθ})[k]|²` — the differentiable objective whose analytic gradient [`phase_gradient`] returns. Exposed so the finite-difference check can perturb `theta` and recompute `L` directly.

symbols.name: receipt
symbols.doc: RVF-style experiment receipt (ADR-260 §15).

symbols.name: verify_receipt
symbols.doc: Check the receipt's **internal consistency** only: recompute the binding digest from the stored fields and compare it to `rvf_receipt_hash`.

symbols.name: replay_and_verify
symbols.signature: fn replay_and_verify(r: &ExperimentReceipt, input: &InputImage, mask: &PhaseMask, config: &OpticalConfig) -> bool
symbols.doc: **Full** verification: re-run the experiment from its inputs and confirm the receipt's bound hashes match a fresh deterministic run — not merely that the stored fields are internally consistent (all [`verify_receipt`] checks).
